title: Unmasking On-Policy Distillation: Where It Helps, Where It Hurts, and Why
gist: Apple researchers introduce a training-free diagnostic that measures, per token and per teacher, whether on-policy distillation actually helps a reasoning model, finding the signal is far more useful on incorrect rollouts than correct ones and that no single distillation configuration is universally optimal.

insights.claim: On-policy distillation provides dense, per-token supervision for training reasoning models, but it remains unclear under which conditions this signal helps versus hurts.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Teams spending compute on distillation to build reasoning models may be paying for signal that is sometimes actively harmful; the choice is not settled best practice.
insights.quote: On-policy distillation offers dense, per-token supervision for training reasoning models; however, it remains unclear under which conditions this signal is beneficial and under which it is detrimental.

insights.claim: Answering which teacher to use, which self-distillation context to use, and whether the optimal choice varies token-to-token currently requires costly training runs whose aggregate metrics obscure token-level dynamics.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: The status quo makes distillation tuning expensive and blind; a cheaper diagnostic would cut wasted training spend.
insights.locator: Which teacher model should be used

insights.claim: The authors introduce a training-free diagnostic framework that operates at per-token, per-question, and per-teacher resolution rather than requiring full training runs.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A cheap pre-training diagnostic could let labs screen distillation setups before committing GPU budget.
insights.quote: We introduce a training-free diagnostic framework that operates at the highest resolution: per token, per question, and per teacher.

insights.claim: They define an ideal per-node gradient as the parameter update that maximally increases the student's probability of success, then estimate it with a scalable targeted-rollout algorithm that works even for long chains of intermediate thoughts.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Gives a concrete, computable reference target against which any real distillation signal can be scored.
insights.locator: We derive an ideal per-node gradient

insights.claim: The gradient alignment score is the cosine similarity between the ideal gradient and any given distillation gradient, quantifying how closely a configuration approximates the ideal signal.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Turns the qualitative question of teacher choice into a single measurable number per configuration.

insights.claim: Distillation guidance aligns substantially better with the ideal signal on incorrect rollouts than on correct ones, because where the student already performs well the teacher's signal tends to become noisy.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Suggests concentrating distillation supervision on failure cases and down-weighting it where the student is already right — a targeted rather than blanket recipe.
insights.quote: distillation guidance exhibits substantially higher alignment with the ideal on incorrect rollouts than on correct ones, where the student already performs well and the teacher's signal tends to become noisy
insights.locator: Across a range of self-distillation settings

insights.claim: The optimal distillation context depends jointly on the student model's capacity and the target task, and no single universally effective configuration emerges.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Undercuts any hope of a one-size-fits-all distillation recipe; motivates per-task, per-token diagnostic analysis before training.
insights.quote: the optimal distillation context depends jointly on the student model's capacity and the target task, and that no single universally effective configuration emerges
insights.locator: Furthermore, we find that the optimal distillation context

insights.claim: A companion distillation scaling law estimates distilled model performance from a compute budget and its allocation between student and teacher, enabling compute-optimal allocation and reducing the risk of large-scale distillation.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Compute-optimal distillation recipes let organizations size student and teacher spend deliberately rather than by trial and error.
insights.quote: We propose a distillation scaling law that estimates distilled model performance based on a compute budget and its allocation between the student and teacher.
